column4: !TENSION:no-effect-plays a card requiring an object for its effect is flatly unplayable without one; pulls against cases elsewhere where a card may be played knowing it will have no effect
classifies Fractured Armament's own basic-mode ability as not a damaging strike; fails transfer — only concerns this card's own two modes; nearest 4.3 doesn't own per-card strike-type determinations
mirror of .2 for the superior mode; same reasoning, card-specific strike classification, no transfer beyond this card
!TENSION:no-effect-plays permissive side: playable even knowing it will have no effect for lack of a target
!TENSION:no-effect-plays prohibition side: card cannot be played if targets are insufficient to allocate all its points
cumulative/stacking effects — nothing owns "multiple instances of an effect stack" (watch-list gap); 1.2 nearest (periodicity) but doesn't claim stacking
sect-as-trait gap (taxonomy watch list cites this exact ruling): nothing owns sect itself or the precedence of a temporary sect override over a permanent one; 5.8/5.7 nearest but don't claim it
Gambit's own VP-transfer-on-withdrawal mechanic; fails transfer — no other card shares this reward structure; nearest 6.5 (pool/VP) doesn't own bespoke card VP effects

confirms Gather only moves a vampire from the uncontrolled to the ready region and cannot rescue from torpor; restates its own printed scope, no transfer to other cards' region-movement rules; nearest 6.7
clarifies Gemini's Mirror's own protection covers only the vampire and his blood, not other cards on him; card-specific scope of "protect"; nearest 1.12 doesn't own protection-scope wording
applies Ghoul Retainer's own printed range restriction to its 1 damage; restates the card's text, no general principle beyond this card
card-specific targeting restriction (excludes uncontrolled-region vampires); other cards interact with that zone differently (eg {Gather}), so no consistent general rule to extract; nearest 6.7
!TENSION:no-effect-plays prohibition side, prevention-card flavor: cannot be used if there is no damage to prevent
sect-as-trait gap, identical wording to Galaric's Legacy.1 (R0741) — second instance in this chunk
confirms this card's crypt-targeting includes the controller's own crypt; reads its own printed text at face value, no established general default-target rule extracted
restricts Goth Band's own counter-moving ability to cards you don't control; card-specific targeting scope, no transfer
confirms Govern the Unaligned carries no "reveal" clause; restates the plain reading of its own text
confirms compatibility of {Left for Dead} with this vampire; pure combo confirmation, no general principle

reads the singular in this card's own search clause as meaning one Kiasyd; generalises only to "singular means one," which fails non-obviousness — any judge knows it; no transfer beyond this card's search
confirms no implicit blood-quantity requirement on this card's own targeting; restates plain reading, no transfer
sect-as-trait gap — whether a minion counts as "Anarch" for other cards' benefit checks; no section owns sect-as-trait, nearest 5.7/5.8; third instance in this chunk

Groundfighting's own list of what its cancel ability reaches; fails transfer — a different cancel card's scope needs its own rulings from its own text; nearest 1.8 ("what cancellation reaches") but doesn't own this card's specific boundary list
same cluster as .1 — card-specific cancel-scope boundary, no transfer to other cancel cards
same cluster as .1
same cluster as .1
same cluster as .1
same cluster as .1
card-specific playability condition (two ready vampires needed to gain blood); restates its own printed requirement, no transfer
card-specific counter-timing on Anarch Convert's own ability sequence; no general principle beyond this card
!TENSION:no-effect-plays duplicate of the Glancing Blow/Hidden Strength prevention-card template

!TENSION:abnormal-entry-requirements this abnormal-entry effect (putting a card directly on a vampire) is blocked by a "cannot have it" prohibition, pulling against the general "abnormal entry ignores requirements" principle (1.6)
reminder that moving blood beyond a vampire's capacity caps at the target and the excess goes to the blood bank; restates a basic capacity rule any fluent judge knows; nearest 5.1
sect-as-trait gap, same cluster as R0741/R0779/R0800 — fourth instance in this chunk
!TENSION:no-effect-plays third copy of the prevention-card no-damage-to-prevent prohibition template
confirms the plain reading of "current action card" (whichever action card is active); restates evident wording, no transfer
